Run Z-score SuStaIn Algorithm

Source: R/run_ZscoreSustain.R
run_ZscoreSustain.Rd

Wrapper for pySuStaIn's ZscoreSustain class, which implements SuStaIn for continuous biomarker data using z-score models.

Usage

run_ZscoreSustain(
  data,
  biomarker_labels,
  N_startpoints = 25,
  N_S_max = 3,
  N_iterations_MCMC = 100000L,
  output_folder = "output",
  dataset_name = "zscore_data",
  use_parallel_startpoints = FALSE,
  seed = 1L,
  ...
)

Arguments

data

matrix or data.frame of biomarker measurements

  • rows = subjects, columns = biomarkers

biomarker_labels

character vector of biomarker names

N_startpoints

integer number of startpoints for optimization (typically 25)

N_S_max

integer maximum number of subtypes

N_iterations_MCMC

integer number of MCMC iterations (typically 1e5 or 1e6)

output_folder

character where to save results

dataset_name

character name for output files

use_parallel_startpoints

logical whether to parallelize optimization

seed

integer random seed for reproducibility

...

additional arguments passed to pySuStaIn$ZscoreSustain()

Value

a list containing SuStaIn results

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Simulated data
set.seed(123)
n_subjects <- 100
n_biomarkers <- 5
data <- matrix(rnorm(n_subjects * n_biomarkers), 
               nrow = n_subjects, ncol = n_biomarkers)
biomarker_labels <- paste0("Biomarker_", 1:n_biomarkers)

results <- run_ZscoreSustain(
  data = data,
  biomarker_labels = biomarker_labels,
  N_startpoints = 10,
  N_S_max = 3,
  N_iterations_MCMC = 1000,
  output_folder = tempdir(),
  dataset_name = "zscore_example"
)
} # }
